mission.title: lima: Use Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler on macOS Ventura
mission.description: Use Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler on macOS Ventura. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current lima setup
steps.description:
Verify your lima version and configuration:
```bash
limactl --version
```
This feature requires a working lima installation.

steps.title: Review lima configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant lima configuration:
I just learned that on macOS Ventura you can register Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler inside a VM running in arm64 mode.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Use Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler on macOS Ventura
steps.description:
> This needs implementing Virtualization.framework support (#889) right?

I don't know; it depends on if we can get VirtioFS support without it.

Or if it will be possible to somehow bridge between the VirtioFS volume and qemu in some other way. I suspect not, as event notification will be a challenge, but it doesn't hurt to try...

I suspect that the binfmt_misc handler is just copying stuff to the Rosetta volume; Rosetta will perform the translation on the host and put the results back into

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Use Rosetta2 as a binfmt_misc handler on macOS Ventura" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> This needs implementing Virtualization.framework support (#889) right?

I don't know; it depends on if we can get VirtioFS support without it.

Or if it will be possible to somehow bridge between the VirtioFS volume and qemu in some other way. I suspect not, as event notification will be a challenge, but it doesn't hurt to try...

prerequisites.description: A working lima installation or development environment.
